methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $4.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 7 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Ultima Genomics, a Series C-stage biotech company founded by Gilad Almogy, is valued at $4.0B post-money following $700M+ in total capital raised. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 8 private-stage companies and 3 public biotech comparables across the Biotech & Health sector.

Two triangulation methods proved applicable. Stage-Progression analysis, applying a typical 1.7x step-up multiplier from Series C to the next funding round, yields $6.8B. Peer-Median analysis across 7 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies—including Insitro ($2.4B, Series C), Colossal Biosciences ($1.5B, Series C), and Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B, Series B)—produces a median of $1.5B, with a range spanning $600M to $3.3B. Public-Comp Anchoring was not applicable due to the absence of disclosed revenue figures. The public sector median P/S multiple of 6.1x across RXRX, TEM, and ONT.L cannot be meaningfully applied without target revenue data.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $1.3B (low) to $7.8B (high), with a median of $4.2B. The current $4.0B valuation aligns closely with the median estimate. Key uncertainties include the applicability of the 1.7x stage multiplier to this specific company trajectory and the significant dispersion within the peer-median approach, which reflects heterogeneity in biotech risk profiles and market conditions at different funding stages.
